desc:
The trail continues in the same old direction - east and west. Following it with
your eyes, you see that it makes a sharp turn to the north a bit further east.
The trail is nearly overgrown, making it obvious that it is not used very often
these days.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
You are still on the trail taking you towards the waiting mountains in the east.
To the north, you can enter some plains that seem to extend very far in all
directions. Looking to the north-east, you see a small cave.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Here, the grassy trail runs just south of a hill. Looking west, you see the
trail continuing very far, but as you look east, you notice that the trail turns
northwards.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
Just east of here the trail bends to the north, westwards however the trail
continues very far. Northeast of here you see a hill, and further in the east
you see an even higher hill, but they are nothing compared to the high mountains
far in the east.
name: Trail to the Misty Mountains

desc:
You are standing on the trail to the mountains as it bends from west to north.
South of here you see a slightly rocky plain, and to the west the trail
continues over the flat landscape. In the east you see a high hill rising over
the trees.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
The slope that is so visible further north is here just a faint slope that is
almost invisible, instead the forest has become a more vivid part of the
scenery, and light forest is now completely surrounding you. East the woods seem
to get a little thicker.
name: Light Forest

desc:
You are standing in the light forest just at the border where it opens up to
some grassy plains to the south. North the forest continues and it even becomes
a little thicker though it is hardly visible. To the north the ground also seems
to rise a little.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The forest bordering the trail is very light, making travel here quite easy. The
forest continues to the east while it leaves way for the trail to the north of
here. The grass on the ground is not very high and not many bushes can be seen.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The light forest here opens up into plains with high grass to the south. East
and west the light forest continues, and the vegetation does not shift much. The
grass is just high enough here to cover your feet when you walk, making the
ground very soft and comfortable.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The plains here are covered with thick grass that reaches almost to your knees
when standing in it. Here and there you see rabbit-holes. And where rabbits are,
foxes are, so you guess a particular large hole at a small hill close to the
border of the forest to the north is a fox-hole.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The plains bordering the forest to the north are covered with thick grass that
covers you all the way to your knees when you stand in it. No trees grow on the
plains, just an occational bush or two, providing shelter for the animals in the
middle of the days.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The light forest here is not as open as the ones west of and south of here, and
further north a thick hedge of bushes prevents travelling further, and the
forest beyond that is much denser than this one. South the ground slopes down
just a little as the forest clears up even more.
name: Light Forest

desc:
This hut has probably not been used in a long time. The roof has partially
fallen in and one of the stone walls has started to crumble. The door and all of
the windows are broken. The floor is covered with all kinds of junk but in one
corner you see a small trapdoor in the floor.
name: Inside a Deserted Hut

desc:
The light forest changes into grassy plains further to the south. North of here
however you see a deserted hut that seem to have been empty for a long time. The
roof has fallen in at one place and all windows on this side are broken and so
is the door. Perhaps it was once the home of a local farmer.
name: Outside a Deserted Hut

desc:
The plains here at the border of the light forest to the north are covered with
thick grass. The plain descends down to a small hole at one place, with a small
pond with some light trees at the edge of the water. This would be an important
place for the animals in the area as the water seem to be very fresh.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The plains at the border of the thin forest to the north are covered with thick
grass that reaches far beyond your ankles. The plains continue to the east and
west; west of here you also see the ground descend down in a hollow, where some
trees also grow.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grassy plains continue to the east and west of here. To the north the plains
slowly turn into a thin forest. The grass growing on the plain is not as thick
here as it is further west, but much taller. The tall grass is a perfect hideout
for larger animals such as deers.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The thin forest is very sparse and slowly fades away into the grassy plains to
the south. North of here the ground slopes down into a ravine which looks like a
dead end. East the thin forest continues and far to the east you see what seems
to be a thick forest.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
The ravine comes to a stop as a huge cliff prevents you from exiting anywhere
but back south. To the south the ground slopes up some and some trees start to
cover the ground. Down here in the ravine the main vegetation is bushes, quite
thick bushes actually, some of them might even have food on them.
name: Ravine

desc:
The forest here is notably lighter than the ones north and east of here. The
vegetation on the ground here is also much richer than that of the surrounding
woods. A small spring comes out from a rock at a small cliff, probably an
underground spring from the hills you see in the north. The spring ends in a
hole in the ground, forming a small pond of fresh water.
name: Light Forest

desc:
This thin forest slowly fades away into grassy plains to the south. East of here
the thin forest becomes a little thicker and further to the east you see a thick
forest that becomes even thicker to the northeast. On the ground the grass grows
thick and that makes the ground very soft and nice to walk on.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
The grassy plains that surround the area are covered with tall grass that would
reach a grown up deer to its belly. To the north the plains transform into a
light forest, and further to the north the forest becomes much darker.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
You are in a place in the forest where it shifts hastily from a thin forest to
the west into a dark black forest a little to the northeast. Right here the
forest is still quite light, and the ground is covered with thick grass, but not
far east and north the forest is much thicker.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The woods become thicker and north and east of here they become even thicker.
West and south of here the forest opens up into light forest and grassy plains.
To the northwest you see some hills rise above the forest, the view from up
there would probably be quite good.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The dense forest here makes a border to the dark forests to the north. To the
east the dense forest continues, but west it opens up into a much lighter one.
The ground is covered with small rocks, making travel a little difficult.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The dense forest surrounds you and makes you feel very small. To the north the
forest grows even thicker. Further to the east you see the Misty Mountains tower
up like a wall, but that is still beyond your reach as the dense forests prevent
further travel to the east.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The dense forest from the south gets even thicker the further north you go. To
the north the forest is so dense it is not even possible to go there, and you
have to turn back or find some other entrance to the forest.
name: Edge of a Dark Forest

desc:
The forest becomes darker and north of here it becomes really dark. To the south
and west however it clears up a little. The ground is quite rocky here, making
it difficult to walk. As you look up in the sky you see high trees with lots of
branches, preventing the sunlight from hitting the ground with full energy.
name: Entering a Black Forest

desc:
This cellar under the deserted hut is in far better shape than the main building
itself. Along one wall stands what is left of some old shelves that seem to have
been a storage place for food and groceries. The shelf covers almost all of the
wall and only leaves a dark corner at the far end. The floor is not of soil but
of stone, making you think that it was not a regular farmer that once lived
here; however, that is only a wild guess.
name: Cellar

desc:
This hidden room was probably the study of some important man. At the wall you
see an old desk and a chair, the desk has a few closed drawers. Fixed to the
wall you see a shield and a longsword. This place has something strange with it,
something evil. You just cannot figure out what, though.
name: Hidden Backroom

desc:
Breaking down the earthwall you enter a hidden chamber behind the wall. The
chamber is big and has lots of dark corners. As you explore the chamber you
sense a strong presence of evil. Maybe you should get out of here while you can.
On the floor you see marks of something having been dragged across the floor.
name: Hidden Chamber

desc:
The tunnel is quite large and you have no problem walking in it, it is dug out
of the earth and has a rounded roof. You suddenly realise what kind of creatures
live in these tunnels as you see huge piles of bones at the end of the tunnel,
and the huge footprints in the ground. Those are troll-tracks, trolls!
name: Earth Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel comes to a bend and continues to the west and south of here. The
tunnel is large and you have no problem standing or walking in it. You wonder
who made theese tunnels, but can not come to a conclusion on that. On the walls
you see a lot of spider-web; some spiders quickly run across the ground as you
enter.
name: Bend in the Earth Tunnel

desc:
Entering the hole under the roots you see an earth tunnel continue to the north.
The tunnel is quite large and would not give you any problem exploring it. The
roof is rounded and the whole tunnel seem to have been dug out by someone - or
something.
name: Earth Tunnel

desc:
This small house consist of only one room, and it is obviously the home of a
hunter of some kind, with heads from all kinds of different beasts set on the
walls. A bed stands in one corner, with an old, and probably useless composite
bow above it. The only exit is through the wooden door to the north.
name: The Hunter's Lodge

desc:
The deserted wasteland spreads around you. The only change in the local scenery
is a small ruin on a hill to the west. No birds live here, and the only sound
you hear, is the rattling of a snake disappearing into the brownish grass.
name: Wasteland

desc:
Nothing, and again nothing exciting can be found in the discouraging view that
meets you as you turn around to have a look at the surrounding land. Wasteland
lies to the north and the south. Moving east and west is prevented by thorny
bushes and thick undergrowth.
name: Wasteland

desc:
This portion of the wasteland must have been forested at one point, as there are
many broken and rotting stumps still clinging to the rocky soil. There are no
trees left intact, however, and the only vegetation to be found are small,
stunted bushes and a few patches of hardy grass and weeds, almost brown from
lack of moisture.
name: Wasteland

desc:
The land here is the same wasteland you have been walking through for far too
long. You notice that there are more bushes here, than there was further back. A
sudden rattle in the bushes makes you spin around, but you see nothing
special... You have a VERY bad feeling about this place.
name: Wasteland

desc:
You seem to have reached the end of the wasteland. Huge blocks prevent any
further travel to the east and the south. However, wasteland lies both north and
west of here.
name: Wasteland

desc:
The hidden cleft under the huge rock is quite narrow and moving around down here
is not that easy. Some other clefts and openings can be found leading out of
here in all directions, but none of them seem to lead anywhere interesting
though, some of them are quite dark and you can never be certain. On the ground
you see some bones from an animal which probably has fallen down into the cleft
and died.
name: Rocky Cleft
